input.description: Weekly essays on economic history for finance professionals and policy people
input.best_edition: The 1970s Inflation We're Not Supposed to Remember
input.testimonial: The only newsletter I read the same day it arrives. — Senior economist

test_cases.output_summary: Full page copy: two headline options (benefit-led, not brand-led), specific subheadline, two-sentence value proposition with genuine differentiator, social proof with real numbers and job-title testimonial, four-bullet what-to-expect section, three CTA button options. 350 words total. No hype language.

input.description: Weekly newsletter for independent food and hospitality professionals — crop economics, supplier relationships, revenue models
input.tone: Direct, energetic, like advice from someone who has made all the mistakes
input.best_edition: How one restaurant doubled event revenue by switching to structured reader dinners

test_cases.output_summary: Two headline options using restaurant-owner language. Value prop distinguishes from general food media. Social proof section honest about no testimonials, suggests what to gather. What-to-expect section has practical specificity about the type of content. CTA options all specific to action.

input.description: Biweekly deep-dives on corporate climate accounting — ESG reporting standards, greenwashing cases, and regulatory changes — for sustainability professionals and financial analysts
input.paid_tier: Paid subscribers get access to a quarterly database of corporate emissions discrepancy cases
input.incentive: Free sample report on ESG reporting divergence for new subscribers

test_cases.output_summary: Headlines lead with professional value for compliance/analyst audience. Value prop clearly differentiates from ESG-cheerleader newsletters. Free sample report prominently featured. Paid tier described concisely without hard sell. What-to-expect section focused on professional use cases. Social proof honest about smaller list size with strong open rate framing.
